notes: Numeric extraction metadata only (Decision 0018): iNES structure facts, the documented NES hardware master palette table, NES palette indices, and tile layout coordinates. This file must never contain ROM bytes, pixel data, audio data, or any other copied game content.

masterPaletteRgb is the widely documented classic NES palette table. Emulator palette variants differ slightly; if frame verification reports uniform color deltas, override locally with --master-palette-file pointing at an ignored cache JSON of 64 [r,g,b] entries.

palettes support two forms. Direct-RGB form: { "rgb": [entry0, entry1, entry2, entry3] } where each entry is null (transparent, sprite color 0) or an exact [r,g,b] triplet. Master-index form: { "backdrop": index-or-null, "colors": [i1, i2, i3] } referencing NES master palette indices. The current overworld palettes use direct RGB sampled from the user's ignored cached reference image PLTE so composed sprites match the reference pixel-exactly. Palette-to-object color-index order is refinable against pixel-frame verification reports.

spriteCompositions tile indices are documented facts from the public Super Mario Bros. disassembly PlayerGraphicsTable and EnemyGraphicsTable (metasprite tile-number tables). Each player row is 4 rows x (left,right) tile bytes for a 16x32 metasprite; small-player rows use $fc (blank) for the top 16x16, so small Mario is the bottom four tiles. Enemy rows are 3 rows x (left,right) for a 16x24 metasprite; the goomba's visible 16x16 is its lower two rows. Right-column tiles that repeat the left tile (e.g. small standing bottom $4f,$4f) are the same tile drawn with a horizontal flip, represented here by flipX. Entry shape: { "id": string, "patternTable": 0|1, "palette": string, "widthTiles": number, "heightTiles": number, "tiles": [{ "index": 0-255, "column": number, "row": number, "flipX"?: boolean }] }.
